text: This exercise is ATW or around the world needle driving. It is done on the foam simulated suture pad, which is also used in the Big Dipper needle drive exercise. This exercise requires you to suture from an outside to in fashion, and the needle throws will be done in a counterclockwise order. You'll start this exercise in the twelve o'clock position. The entry site is designated by the flashing gray dot and the exit will be also highlighted. Once you have appropriately punctured the correct designated site, you'll see the circle turn green and same thing once you've come through the correct exit site.

I found that this tissue is a little more forgiving in terms of excessive force and tissue penalties on the pull through compared to some of the other suture exercises. But it's still very important to make sure you follow the curve and the needle out on your pull through without any excessive force in order to avoid those penalties. You can see that based on the orientation of the throw, we are alternating between the left and right needle driver. And this is where knowing how your wrist articulation works and planning your next throw is important and vital to moving swiftly through this exercise. You see on this pull through here, we are anticipating having to use the left arm to perform the next throw. And so therefore we are setting up our needle in the correct position as well as grabbing the needle in the pronated position with the left hand in order to make an easy successful throw.

The surgeon uses the left needle driver to pull the suture through the tissue, following the curve of the needle. The needle is then aligned horizontally and loaded with the right needle driver for the next throw.
